generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time temperature readings from building zones for energy management and comfort optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Detects occupancy levels in building spaces to enable demand-based HVAC and lighting control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures relative humidity levels to support indoor air quality monitoring and HVAC optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACSystemStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks operational status of HVAC components (e.g., chiller, AHU) for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingSystemStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors lighting system performance and energy consumption for optimization strategies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects vibration and stress data from structural elements for integrity monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time energy consumption across building systems for demand analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Continuous, event-driven transmission of temperature readings with 100ms latency for critical paths."
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data stream with preprocessing for noise filtering and normalization."
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
    base:desc "High-frequency humidity measurements with validation against baseline environmental conditions."
]

instance HVACStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACSystemStatusSensor
    base:desc "Operational logs and performance metrics from HVAC components with semantic enrichment from BIM."
]

instance LightingStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingSystemStatusSensor
    base:desc "Lighting system telemetry with integration to BIM spatial hierarchies for zone-level control."
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
    base:desc "Structural monitoring data with time-series analysis capabilities for anomaly detection."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
    base:desc "Granular energy consumption data with automatic aggregation for building zones and systems."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMArchitecturalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static architectural representation enriched with semantic annotations for BCS components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BIMMechanicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mechanical systems model with detailed HVAC and plumbing components for simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance BIMElectricalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Electrical systems model including lighting, power distribution, and safety systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlEnabler
]

instance RealTimeBuildingPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated runtime data from all sensing components with temporal alignment to BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, HVACStatusDataTransmitted, LightingStatusDataTransmitted, StructuralHealthDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalBuildingPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data with versioned snapshots for trend analysis and predictive modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Strategies for harmonizing BIM models with runtime data using IFC and semantic alignment."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based and data-driven simulation engines supporting energy performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService, SimulationService
]

instance ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular control logic implementation with safety constraints and dynamic actuation ranges."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data fusion architecture handling heterogeneous streams with 100ms latency."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance AnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning pipelines for predictive maintenance and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance ScalabilityEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Microservices architecture supporting decentralized DT instances with cloud-native deployment."
]

instance IoTDataIngestionPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Collects and preprocesses real-time data from sensors and devices with seamless integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance AIMLAnalyticsLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Powers predictive analytics and optimization algorithms with trained models on historical data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance CollaborationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supports multi-stakeholder interaction with shared workspaces for design reviews."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance DigitalTwinPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified environment for building, managing, and deploying Digital Twins with pre-built modules."
]

instance IFCStandard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables interoperability between BIM tools and Digital Twin components with standardized data exchange."
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMIntegrationEnabler
]

instance OPCUAProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates secure, platform-independent communication between IoT devices and Digital Twin systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationEnabler
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of physical asset performance with live visualization and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, EquipmentAlertAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting future states using historical and real-time data for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceForecastInsight, EnergyConsumptionPredictionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for improving efficiency and reducing operational costs."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendationInsight, SpaceUtilizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual testing of design changes and operational scenarios with impact assessment."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignImpactInsight, ResilienceScenarioInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to physical systems based on Digital Twin insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACControlAction, LightingAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D representations with real-time data overlays and collaborative tools."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveBuildingVisualizationInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Actionable insights for strategic and tactical decisions with risk assessment capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides RiskAssessmentInsight, ComplianceReportInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational metrics including energy usage, occupancy levels, and equipment health."
]

]

    base:desc "Predictive maintenance scheduling with 90% confidence intervals for component failures."
]

]

]

instance SpaceUtilizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized space allocation recommendations based on occupancy patterns and usage trends."
]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Dynamic lighting control with occupancy-based dimming and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "3D BIM-integrated environment with real-time data overlays and AR/VR capabilities."
]

instance RiskAssessmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive risk assessment dashboard with compliance reporting capabilities."
]

instance ComplianceReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated generation of regulatory compliance reports with audit trails."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Modular control logic for HVAC systems with dynamic actuation ranges and safety constraints."
]

instance LightingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Remote control interface for lighting systems with zone-level management capabilities."
]

instance EmergencyResponseSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated emergency response coordination with fail-safe mechanisms."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system enables **real-time control commands** from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed by **Facility Managers** via the deployed DT interface. Command types include **adjustments to HVAC systems, lighting controls, security protocols, and other IoT-enabled devices**, with message formats adhering to **standardized control protocols** (not explicitly specified but implied to be compatible with BIM and facility management tools). Validation and authentication mechanisms are embedded within the framework to ensure **authorized access and command integrity**, while feedback systems provide **closed-loop confirmation** (e.g., acknowledgment of command execution or error notifications). Conflict resolution is managed through **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic prioritization and adjustment of commands without full system redeployment. Command execution confirmation is achieved via **bidirectional data exchange**, where the DT verifies physical system responses against expected outcomes."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling dynamic operational monitoring and control. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms intervals), the requirement implies **low-latency bidirectional data flow** to maintain operational fidelity. Temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing adjustments without redeployment, suggesting adaptability to varying service time scales (e.g., sensor data aggregation, event-driven updates, or periodic analytics). Latency requirements are not explicitly quantified but are inferred to be critical for **interactive visualization** and **real-time monitoring** by Facility Managers, aligning with the AECO domain’s need for immediate feedback loops. The system’s modular architecture further supports **service-specific time scales**, where custom analytics or control services may operate at distinct frequencies (e.g., high-frequency sensor data vs. low-frequency maintenance logs)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated constellation architecture** centered on a **modular, service-oriented framework** with the following key patterns:
  - **Role-Based Orchestration**: The system is structured around **three primary roles** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and a **central DT Framework**, each contributing to the Digital Twin life cycle. This **role-based orchestration** ensures **divided responsibilities** while maintaining system-wide coherence.
  - **Component Integration**: The framework supports **bidirectional data exchange** between **enriched BIM models** (static representations) and **operational Digital Twins** (dynamic counterparts), facilitated by the **modular architecture’s standardized extension points**. This enables **plug-and-play integration** of custom services (e.g., predictive analytics, energy optimization) without disrupting existing workflows.
  - **Resource Management**: The system prioritizes **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **maintainability** (via low-overhead updates), implying **dynamic resource allocation** to handle varying loads (e.g., peak monitoring periods). The **runtime configuration management** feature further optimizes resource use by allowing **on-the-fly adjustments** to service deployments.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The **DT Framework** acts as a **central hub** for **holistic life cycle management**, ensuring synchronization between physical and digital assets. Coordination mechanisms include:
    - **Data Flow Control**: Real-time synchronization between BIM and operational DTs.
    - **Service Mediation**: Custom services (e.g., analytics) are integrated and mediated through the framework’s extension points.
    - **Accessibility Layers**: The system supports **users with varying technical expertise**, implying **abstraction layers** for role-specific interactions (e.g., Facility Managers use simplified interfaces, while DT Engineers configure advanced services)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans **five key phases**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. These phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional attributes, connectivity, and control logic). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., executable models, configuration files, or simulation templates) with **minimal manual intervention**. This phase bridges the static BIM schema and dynamic DT requirements.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to the physical infrastructure, enabling **real-time synchronization** between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase includes **initialization of control logic**, **device calibration**, and **baseline state synchronization** to ensure operational parity.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates in **real-time**, with continuous **bidirectional data exchange** between the physical system and its digital counterpart. Facility Managers interact with the twin via **immersive visualization interfaces** (e.g., 3D BIM-integrated dashboards) to monitor system states (e.g., sensor readings, actuator statuses) and execute control actions (e.g., adjusting HVAC settings).
  5. **Evolution and Extension**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via the framework’s **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**. This phase supports **incremental updates** to the DT, accommodating evolving operational requirements (e.g., new analytics, predictive maintenance modules, or regulatory compliance checks).
  **Transition Management**: Phase transitions are governed by **automated workflows** (e.g., validation checks, dependency resolution) and **role-based access controls** (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for monitoring). **Lifecycle governance** is ensured through **version control** of BIM/DT artifacts and **audit trails** for changes to control logic or device configurations. The framework’s **modularity** allows for **parallel development** of DT components (e.g., separate pipelines for device modeling and service integration)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **model fidelity** through **enriched BIM representations** that bridge the gap between static BIM models and dynamic DT requirements. **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **model-driven deployment process**, where structured representations extracted via **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles ensure semantic consistency and operational accuracy. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated through **real-time data synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital twins, reducing discrepancies caused by delays or data loss. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are supported by the framework’s **modular architecture**, allowing custom services to integrate **anomaly detection** and **automated correction protocols**. **Quality assurance** is enforced through **standardized extension points** for integrating domain-specific validation tools and **runtime monitoring** of DT artifacts, ensuring adherence to operational requirements throughout the DT lifecycle."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **heterogeneous interoperability** across building systems, leveraging a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**. Communication protocols are not explicitly named but must align with **BIM tool integrations (e.g., IFC, COBie)** and **IoT/building automation standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or BACnet)** for seamless data exchange. The network topology follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke model**, where the **DT Framework acts as the central hub**, facilitating bidirectional data flows between physical devices, BIM models, and user interfaces. Bandwidth and latency requirements are optimized for **real-time operational needs**, with **scalability mechanisms** ensuring performance across individual components to entire infrastructure systems. Reliability and redundancy are addressed through **low-overhead maintainability features** and **runtime configuration adjustments**, while security protocols (not detailed) are implied to protect data integrity and access control."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **hybrid hosting models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** options, tailored to the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) domain. Computational and storage requirements are dynamically allocated based on **runtime configuration management**, enabling **scalability** from individual building components to entire infrastructure systems. The framework adopts a **modular deployment model**, allowing **custom service integration** and **low-overhead updates** without full redeployment. Availability and reliability are assured through **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** addressing maintainability and accessibility, with **enriched BIM models** serving as the foundational data layer. Deployment is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools, followed by **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed DT for real-time operations, while **DT Engineers** extend capabilities via service integration."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is designed for **horizontal integration** with external systems through a **requirements-driven, interoperable architecture**, focusing on the following technical aspects:
  - **Data Exchange Protocols**: While not explicitly named, the **real-time data exchange capabilities** imply adherence to **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or REST APIs) for bidirectional communication between the Digital Twin framework and external systems (e.g., IoT sensors, BIM tools, or enterprise asset management systems).
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The system addresses **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a **non-functional requirement (NF_X)**, suggesting compliance with **industry standards** such as **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) for BIM data** and **common data models** for AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) domains. The **enriched BIM models** serve as a **unified data backbone**, enabling seamless integration with external tools.
  - **Dependencies on External Systems**: The framework relies on:
    - **BIM Tools** (e.g., Autodesk Revit, ArchiCAD) for **device modeling and initial deployment** by Building Control Engineers.
    - **IoT/Building Automation Systems** (e.g., BAS, smart sensors) for **real-time operational data**.
    - **Enterprise Systems** (e.g., ERP, CMMS) for **asset management and maintenance workflows**.
  - **Integration Patterns**: The **modular architecture** supports **loose coupling** between the Digital Twin framework and external systems, allowing **plug-and-play integration** of third-party services. The **standardized extension points** enable **service-oriented integration**, where external systems can expose APIs or data feeds to the DT Framework. For example:
    - **BIM-to-DT Integration**: Enriched BIM models are translated into operational Digital Twins, ensuring **semantic consistency** between static and dynamic representations.
    - **DT-to-Enterprise Integration**: Operational data from the Digital Twin can be pushed to **enterprise systems** for reporting or decision-making, leveraging **event-driven or batch-based data exchange** mechanisms.
  The system’s **scalability** and **accessibility** requirements further ensure that horizontal integration does not introduce bottlenecks, supporting **cross-system collaboration** in the AECO domain."
]
